## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Hampshire&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Education")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2f/Baker-Library-Dartmouth-College-Hanover-New-Hampshire-05-2018a.jpg/250px-Baker-Library-Dartmouth-College-Hanover-New-Hampshire-05-2018a.jpg)](/wiki/File:Baker-Library-Dartmouth-College-Hanover-New-Hampshire-05-2018a.jpg)

[Dartmouth College](/wiki/Dartmouth_College "Dartmouth College")'s [Baker Library](/wiki/Baker_Memorial_Library "Baker Memorial Library")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0a/Thompson_Hall%2C_UNH_Sunset.jpg/250px-Thompson_Hall%2C_UNH_Sunset.jpg)](/wiki/File:Thompson_Hall,_UNH_Sunset.jpg)

Thompson Hall, at [UNH](/wiki/University_of_New_Hampshire "University of New Hampshire"), was built in 1892.

New Hampshire’s education system includes public school districts, private schools, and a variety of school choice initiatives. One significant component of the latter is the [New Hampshire Education Freedom Accounts](/wiki/New_Hampshire_Education_Freedom_Accounts "New Hampshire Education Freedom Accounts") program, a statewide initiative that allows eligible families to direct a portion of state education funding toward approved educational expenses outside of traditional public schools.

The average cost per pupil for public K-12 education in New Hampshire exceeded $21,000, with most funding coming from local property taxes.[[128]](#cite_note-133) In 2025, the state expanded Education Freedom Accounts into a universal school choice program, making most students eligible to participate.[[129]](#cite_note-134)[[130]](#cite_note-135)

### High schools

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Hampshire&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: High schools")]

See also: [List of high schools in New Hampshire](/wiki/List_of_high_schools_in_New_Hampshire "List of high schools in New Hampshire") and [List of school districts in New Hampshire](/wiki/List_of_school_districts_in_New_Hampshire "List of school districts in New Hampshire")

The first public high schools in the state were the Boys' High School and the Girls' High School of [Portsmouth](/wiki/Portsmouth,_New_Hampshire "Portsmouth, New Hampshire"), established either in 1827 or 1830, depending on the source.[[131]](#cite_note-136)[[132]](#cite_note-137)[[133]](#cite_note-138)

New Hampshire has more than 80 public high schools, many of which serve more than one town. The largest is [Pinkerton Academy](/wiki/Pinkerton_Academy "Pinkerton Academy") in [Derry](/wiki/Derry,_New_Hampshire "Derry, New Hampshire"), which is owned by a private non-profit organization and serves as the public high school of several neighboring towns. There are at least 30 private high schools in the state.

New Hampshire is also the home of several prestigious [university-preparatory](/wiki/University-preparatory_school "University-preparatory school") schools, such as [Phillips Exeter Academy](/wiki/Phillips_Exeter_Academy "Phillips Exeter Academy"), [St. Paul's School](/wiki/St._Paul%27s_School_(New_Hampshire) "St. Paul's School (New Hampshire)"), [Proctor Academy](/wiki/Proctor_Academy "Proctor Academy"), [Brewster Academy](/wiki/Brewster_Academy "Brewster Academy"), and [Kimball Union Academy](/wiki/Kimball_Union_Academy "Kimball Union Academy").

Traditionally, school funding has been decided by municipalities, often at [town meeting](/wiki/Town_meeting "Town meeting"). In 1993, the [*Claremont* litigation](/wiki/Claremont_School_District_v._Governor_of_New_Hampshire "Claremont School District v. Governor of New Hampshire") began when that town's school district secured a verdict that Part 2, Article 83 of the state constitution required the state to fund education adequately and uniformly. This was never achieved despite additional rounds of litigation. In 2002, New Hampshire enacted a [statewide property tax](/wiki/Government_of_New_Hampshire#Funding "Government of New Hampshire") to be forwarded to school districts to address the problem. In 2008, the Court ended its supervisory role because the original laws had been replaced, but it did not reverse its earlier finding.[[134]](#cite_note-139) In 2023, a district judge set a new, higher amount for educational "adequacy". In June 2025, the legislature enacted language asserting its right to determine funding. The New Hampshire Supreme Court upheld the 2023 verdict but declined to force the legislature to comply.[[135]](#cite_note-BG-140)

Annually since 2002, high-school statewide all-stars compete against Vermont in 10 sports during "Twin State" playoffs.[[141]](#cite_note-146)

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Hampshire&action=edit&section=34 "Edit section: Culture")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/GAUDENS_I.jpg/250px-GAUDENS_I.jpg)](/wiki/File:GAUDENS_I.jpg)

Detail of a reproduction of *[Diana](/wiki/Diana_(Saint-Gaudens) "Diana (Saint-Gaudens)")* at the [Saint-Gaudens National Historical Park](/wiki/Saint-Gaudens_National_Historical_Park "Saint-Gaudens National Historical Park"). New Hampshire's sole [national park](/wiki/National_Park_Service "National Park Service") is one of only two dedicated to a [visual artist](/wiki/Visual_arts "Visual arts").

In the spring, New Hampshire's many [sap houses](/wiki/Sugar_house "Sugar house") hold sugaring-off open houses. In summer and early autumn, New Hampshire is home to many [county fairs](/wiki/Fair "Fair"), the largest being the [Hopkinton State Fair](/wiki/Hopkinton_State_Fair "Hopkinton State Fair"), in [Contoocook](/wiki/Contoocook,_New_Hampshire "Contoocook, New Hampshire"). New Hampshire's [Lakes Region](/wiki/Lakes_Region_(New_Hampshire) "Lakes Region (New Hampshire)") is home to many summer camps, especially around [Lake Winnipesaukee](/wiki/Lake_Winnipesaukee "Lake Winnipesaukee"), and is a popular tourist destination. The Peterborough Players have performed every summer in [Peterborough](/wiki/Peterborough,_New_Hampshire "Peterborough, New Hampshire") since 1933. [The Barnstormers Theatre](/wiki/The_Barnstormers_Theatre "The Barnstormers Theatre") in [Tamworth](/wiki/Tamworth,_New_Hampshire "Tamworth, New Hampshire"), founded in 1931, is one of the longest-running professional summer theaters in the United States.[[142]](#cite_note-147) The [Saint-Gaudens National Historical Park](/wiki/Saint-Gaudens_National_Historical_Park "Saint-Gaudens National Historical Park") is the sole [National Park Unit](/wiki/National_Park_Service "National Park Service") in the state, and a section of the [Appalachian Trail](/wiki/Appalachian_Trail "Appalachian Trail") passes through the north.

In September, New Hampshire is host to the *New Hampshire Highland Games*. New Hampshire has also registered an official [tartan](/wiki/Tartan "Tartan") with the proper authorities in [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"), used to make kilts worn by the [Lincoln](/wiki/Lincoln,_New_Hampshire "Lincoln, New Hampshire") Police Department while its officers serve during the games. The [fall foliage](/wiki/Autumn_leaf_color "Autumn leaf color") peaks in mid-October. In the winter, [New Hampshire's ski areas](/wiki/List_of_ski_areas_and_resorts_in_the_United_States#New_Hampshire "List of ski areas and resorts in the United States") and [snowmobile](/wiki/Snowmobile "Snowmobile") trails attract visitors from a wide area.[[143]](#cite_note-148) After the lakes freeze over they become dotted with [ice fishing](/wiki/Ice_fishing "Ice fishing") ice houses, known locally as bobhouses.

[Funspot](/wiki/Funspot_Family_Fun_Center "Funspot Family Fun Center"), the world's largest video arcade[[144]](#cite_note-Largest_videogame_arcade_(current)_–_Guinness_World_Records-149)[[145]](#cite_note-150) (now termed a museum), is in [Laconia](/wiki/Laconia,_New_Hampshire "Laconia, New Hampshire").

### In fiction

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Hampshire&action=edit&section=35 "Edit section: In fiction")]

Many [novels](/wiki/Category:Novels_set_in_Hampshire "Category:Novels set in Hampshire"), [plays](/wiki/Category:Plays_set_in_New_Hampshire "Category:Plays set in New Hampshire") and [screenplays](/wiki/Category:Films_set_in_New_Hampshire "Category:Films set in New Hampshire") have been set in New Hampshire.

#### Theater

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Hampshire&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Theater")]

* The fictional New Hampshire town of Grover's Corners serves as the setting of the [Thornton Wilder](/wiki/Thornton_Wilder "Thornton Wilder") play *[Our Town](/wiki/Our_Town "Our Town")*. Grover's Corners is based, in part, on the real town of [Peterborough](/wiki/Peterborough,_New_Hampshire "Peterborough, New Hampshire"). Several local landmarks and nearby towns are mentioned in the text of the play, and Wilder himself spent some time in Peterborough at the [MacDowell Colony](/wiki/MacDowell_Colony "MacDowell Colony"), writing at least some of the play while in residence there.[[146]](#cite_note-151)

#### Comics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Hampshire&action=edit&section=37 "Edit section: Comics")]

* Al Capp, creator of the comic strip *[Li'l Abner](/wiki/Li%27l_Abner "Li'l Abner")*, used to joke that [Dogpatch](/wiki/Dogpatch "Dogpatch"), the setting for the strip, was based on [Seabrook](/wiki/Seabrook,_New_Hampshire "Seabrook, New Hampshire"), where he would vacation with his wife.[[147]](#cite_note-152)

#### Television

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Hampshire&action=edit&section=38 "Edit section: Television")]

Many [television programs](/wiki/Category:Television_shows_set_in_New_Hampshire "Category:Television shows set in New Hampshire") have been set in New Hampshire. Notable recent examples include:

* In the AMC drama *[Breaking Bad](/wiki/Breaking_Bad "Breaking Bad")* ("[Granite State](/wiki/Granite_State_(Breaking_Bad) "Granite State (Breaking Bad)")"[[148]](#cite_note-:3-153)) series lead [Walter White](/wiki/Walter_White_(Breaking_Bad) "Walter White (Breaking Bad)") escapes to a cabin in a fictional county in northern New Hampshire.
* An episode of the NBC drama *[The West Wing](/wiki/The_West_Wing "The West Wing")* takes place in the fictional [Hartsfield's Landing](/wiki/Hartsfield%27s_Landing "Hartsfield's Landing"), New Hampshire.
* In the sixth season of [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") series *[The Sopranos](/wiki/The_Sopranos "The Sopranos")*, in an episode named for New Hampshire's slogan "[Live Free or Die](/wiki/Live_Free_or_Die "Live Free or Die")", a character flees to the fictional town of Dartford, New Hampshire, because of inadvertently being [outed](/wiki/Outing "Outing") as a gay man.[[149]](#cite_note-Live_Free_or_Die_–_Season_6_Ep._6,_The_Sopranos,_HBO-154)

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Hampshire&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: Notable people")]

For a more comprehensive list, see [List of people from New Hampshire](/wiki/List_of_people_from_New_Hampshire "List of people from New Hampshire").

Prominent individuals from New Hampshire include 14th President of the United States [Franklin Pierce](/wiki/Franklin_Pierce "Franklin Pierce"), [founding father](/wiki/Founding_Fathers_of_the_United_States "Founding Fathers of the United States") [Nicholas Gilman](/wiki/Nicholas_Gilman "Nicholas Gilman"), Senator [Daniel Webster](/wiki/Daniel_Webster "Daniel Webster"), [Revolutionary War](/wiki/American_Revolutionary_War "American Revolutionary War") hero [John Stark](/wiki/John_Stark "John Stark"), editor [Horace Greeley](/wiki/Horace_Greeley "Horace Greeley"), founder of the [Christian Science](/wiki/Christian_Science "Christian Science") religion [Mary Baker Eddy](/wiki/Mary_Baker_Eddy "Mary Baker Eddy"), poet [Robert Frost](/wiki/Robert_Frost "Robert Frost"), sculptor [Daniel Chester French](/wiki/Daniel_Chester_French "Daniel Chester French"), astronaut [Alan Shepard](/wiki/Alan_Shepard "Alan Shepard"), rock musician [Ronnie James Dio](/wiki/Ronnie_James_Dio "Ronnie James Dio"), author [Dan Brown](/wiki/Dan_Brown "Dan Brown"), actor-comedian [Adam Sandler](/wiki/Adam_Sandler "Adam Sandler"), inventor [Dean Kamen](/wiki/Dean_Kamen "Dean Kamen"), comedians [Sarah Silverman](/wiki/Sarah_Silverman "Sarah Silverman") and [Seth Meyers](/wiki/Seth_Meyers "Seth Meyers"), restaurateurs [Richard and Maurice McDonald](/wiki/Richard_and_Maurice_McDonald "Richard and Maurice McDonald"), WWE wrestler [Triple H](/wiki/Triple_H "Triple H"), and streamer [Ludwig Ahgren](/wiki/Ludwig_Ahgren "Ludwig Ahgren").
